human: The Treaty of Westphalia, signed in 1648, fundamentally restructured European political order. It established the principle of state sovereignty, ending decades of religious conflict. Historians regard it as the foundation of the modern nation-state system, though its precise legacy remains contested among scholars.

Marie Curie was born in Warsaw in 1867. She moved to Paris to study physics and chemistry at the Sorbonne. In 1903 she shared the Nobel Prize in Physics with her husband Pierre and Henri Becquerel, becoming the first woman to win one. She later won a second Nobel, this time in Chemistry, in 1911.
